body_txt:
Dating as a single parent can be difficult for multitude of reasons. Sometimes your kids reject your partner and sometimes your partner turns out to be a Wicked Step-parent . Other times, just getting a second (or even first) date is hard because people run away after hearing about your kids. They either aren't ready for kids yet (assuming they want kids at all ), don't want to deal with the baggage of being a step-parent , don't want to raise children who are not biological, or believe that the single parent just wants a Meal Ticket to financially support them and their kids (this one is aimed mostly towards single mothers since women are often said to care more about their partner's income than men do and single mothers are more likely to be impoverished than single fathers). This is often Truth in Television , as many single parents can attest. As a result, many single parents date other single parents. It is especially difficult for single parents who are under the age of 30 to find a partner, since many people in their teens and twenties are not able or willing to commit to such a big responsibility so early in their lives. Plus, there are still plenty of potential partners within that age group who don't have children yet (which also makes the "dating another single parent option" more challenging), so they don't have to make compromises and "settle". Compare to Struggling Single Mother , for when a single parent struggles financially, and Old Maid , which deals with women who can't find a date because of age instead of parenthood.
Contrast with Marry the Nanny , in which a person meets their future spouse because of their kids. If finding out that their potential spouse is also a devoted parent makes the person even more interested, the trope may overlap with Single Woman Seeks Good Man . See also Kids Play Match Breaker when the children take a more proactive role in chasing away suitors.
